name: ranger_patrol
notes: Fire effectiveness from Lindsey et al. (2018): patrol density linked to 15-25% reduction in fire incidence through early detection and suppression. Vegetation effectiveness (0.15) is an estimate reflecting illegal clearance deterrence; no direct citation.

name: firebreak_maintenance
citation: FAO (2006) Fire Management: Global Assessment. FAO Forestry Paper 151. Rome.
notes: Fire effectiveness from FAO (2006): firebreaks reduce fire spread by 40-50% in African savanna ecosystems. Cost per km from FAO field implementation data.

name: controlled_burn
citation: FAO (2006) Fire Management: Global Assessment. FAO Forestry Paper 151. Rome.
notes: Fire effectiveness from FAO (2006): prescribed burning reduces fuel load and lowers subsequent wildfire probability by 30-40%. Cost per hectare from FAO Africa implementation data.

name: waterhole_maintenance
citation: AWF (2019) Conservation Finance in African Protected Areas. African Wildlife Foundation.
notes: Drought effectiveness from AWF (2019): waterhole maintenance sustains wildlife water access and reduces drought-related habitat abandonment by approximately 40-50%. Vegetation effectiveness (0.20) is an estimate reflecting improved riparian vegetation around maintained water sources; no direct citation.

name: community_awareness
citation: USAID (2020) Community-Based Natural Resource Management in West Africa. USAID West Africa.
notes: Fire effectiveness from USAID (2020): community fire reporting and prevention programs reduce fire incidence by approximately 10-20% in participating communities. Vegetation effectiveness (0.10) is an estimate reflecting reduced illegal harvesting pressure; no direct citation.

name: vegetation_monitoring
notes: All effectiveness values are estimates. Vegetation monitoring enables early detection of degradation events and timely management response; 0.30 reflects assumed probability reduction from early intervention. Cost is approximate based on field sensor and transect survey rates in West Africa. Flagged for sensitivity analysis.
